stimulus:
Welcome!

In this study, you will play three short video games that were generated by Artificial Intelligence (AI) and complete a short survey after each game.

Please make sure you are in a quiet environment where you can focus for the duration of the study. Some games may use sound, so please ensure that your device's volume is not muted.

Press any key to begin.
Press any key to proceed to the first video game. Should you encounter a game-breaking bug, please report the issue in the Menu tab to the right of the screen.

response: {"creativity-rating":3,"creativity-free-response":"It felt like electroplankton and I'm sure there are other similar games about launching things and creating sound, so not super creative. It was also kind of confusing and difficult, so... to me, to be creative it needs to also be good (being different at random doesn't make it creative)","originality-rating":3,"originality-free-response":"As I said, it felt a lot like electroplankton in a way (the good way of making sound by launching stuff). The difference was the objective. I never felt like I had control, which is original, but not good.","effectiveness-rating":1,"effectiveness-free-response":"I felt like I didn't have any control, I was launching the things but I wasn't able to make them go anywhere near where I wanted (like, I couldn't make them gravitate, for example). Thus, I couldn't produce any sounds I wanted to produce and it felt random. I just launched a lot to clear the game, I couldn't \"match\" anything really.","authenticity-rating":4,"attention-check":3,"intentionality-rating":4,"authenticity-intentionality-free-response":"Even though I thought of Elektroplankton, I haven't seen a game that combined that with gravity or with these objectives. I think that's the developer's idea, though I suppose they thought it would be more fun."}

stimulus:

response: {"creativity-rating":2,"creativity-free-response":"It feels like one of the simplest ideas for a game, it's not fun or entertaining.","originality-rating":1,"originality-free-response":"I've seen this game (or a variation) many times, it's a very simple game, people make it at global game jam a lot. ","effectiveness-rating":1,"effectiveness-free-response":"In the realm of echo visibility games, this was probably one of the worst I've ever played. The echo started where I was when I moved, so it felt like it was lagging behind me, it didn't illuminate much. Some walls seemed unpassable, too. It was like a forced \"labyrinth\" but also it wasn't even interesting, I \"solved\" it in one try (did it even have something to stop me?). There was a triangle that seemed to do nothing.","authenticity-rating":1,"attention-check":3,"intentionality-rating":3,"authenticity-intentionality-free-response":"As I mentioned, I've seen this game so many times I'm sure everyone knows one or another version, so I don't think there's anything authentic. What I'm not sure, however, is how the developer imagined this, maybe it's not their fault and the AI made it super basic (in other words, there can be a game with a known mechanic that is original and good, but this is the most basic version of this kind of game)."}
